tool: headless Chromium via Playwright (Chrome MCP unavailable — documented fallback)
sanitization: Composer form pre-filled with fake demo values (name demo-mission, repo github.com/acme/demo-app, live_url demo.example.com); no real operator data, email addresses, session tokens, or other PII appear in any frame.

entries.note: Guide names a separate 'Constraints' field; on the live composer constraints are expressed via the acceptance-criteria list (+ Add criterion / Skip). The Goal capture region (mission field + acceptance list) is present and was captured.

Guide names an 'Accept draft' button; the live composer drafts via AI assist and commits with Inject into mission / Enqueue. The Mission queue panel (Enqueue, Priority order) is present and was captured.

entries.reason: The guide depicts a 'Start run' button on a mission-control screen. The live composer has no such control - a mission is committed with Enqueue / Save & Launch and started by the queue runner (Pause/Resume live on /queue). No 1:1 UI exists to capture, so no image was written.

The guide depicts a mission-timeline panel with a per-iteration build / deploy-gate / test breakdown. The live /dashboard surfaces an Active runs table (Mission/Status/Iter/Streak/Cost) but no per-iteration breakdown, and its runs data is control-plane-gated: an unauthenticated render shows a data-load error and an authenticated render would expose real operator run data (PII). No clean, sanitized timeline is capturable, so no image was written.
